# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 86

This is turn 86. Turns 1 through 85 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 86.

## Turn 86 — the fixed-frame proof as one theorem

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In all mathematical arguments the displayed exponent denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\in\widehat{\mathbb Z}\); \(40491355905\) is only its required residue modulo \(85667662080\). 

There is one wording correction to Turn 85. The final proof should invoke the **direct small-exponent form** of Thompson’s critical-subgroup theorem. It should not claim, without a separate argument, that this form follows merely by replacing an arbitrary critical subgroup \(C\) by \(\Omega _2(C)\). The precise external input needed is:

> For every finite \(2\)-group \(K\), there is a characteristic subgroup \(D\le K\) such that
> \[
> D^4=1,\qquad [D,K]\le Z(D),
> \]
> and the kernel of
> \[
> \operatorname{Aut}(K)\longrightarrow\operatorname{Aut}(D)
> \]
> is a \(2\)-group.

Equivalently, every odd-order automorphism group acting nontrivially on \(K\) acts nontrivially on \(D\). This is exactly the proposition used in the critical-block argument; no stronger hybrid form is needed. 

### Fixed-tame-frame theorem

Let \((G,L,\theta)\) be a finite decorated marked target, where \(L\triangleleft G\) is a \(2\)-group and \(\theta:G\to E\) is an elementary-abelian scalar decoration. Fix both a decoration value \(\psi\) and a complete tame frame
\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow G/L.
\]

For either source
\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_{A_2},G_{\mathbb Q_2}\},
\]
write
\[
N_\Gamma(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi)
\]
for the number of surjections inducing \(\alpha\), satisfying the decoration, and sending the candidate wild subgroup—or local wild inertia—into \(L\).

Then
\[
N_{\Gamma_{A_2}}(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi)
=
N_{G_{\mathbb Q_2}}(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi).
\tag{86.1}
\]

### Proof by induction on \(\lvert L\rvert\)

If every \(G\)-chief factor of \(L\) is trivial, the terminal scalar theorem expresses \(G\) as a fibre product of the fixed tame quotient with a finite \(2\)-group. The count is then determined by the common maximal pro-\(2\) quotient
\[
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\ 
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}.
\]
This is the base case.

Otherwise choose
\[
S\triangleleft P\triangleleft G
\]
so that the factors below \(S\) are scalar and
\[
P/S\simeq V
\]
is the first nontrivial simple factor. Choose \(K\triangleleft G\), \(K\le P\), minimally subject to \(KS=P\).

Let \(N\) be the odd part of the fixed tame quotient that acts nontrivially on \(V\), and choose an odd Hall lift \(\widetilde N\) in \(G\). Because all chief factors below \(S\) are trivial, the standard coprime-series lemma gives
\[
[S,\widetilde N]=1.
\]
But \(\widetilde N\) acts nontrivially on
\[
K/(K\cap S)\simeq V,
\]
and hence nontrivially on \(K\).

Apply the direct small-exponent critical-subgroup theorem to \(K\). Its characteristic subgroup \(D\) cannot be centralized by \(\widetilde N\), so \(D\nleq S\). Since \(D\triangleleft G\), its image in the simple module \(P/S\) is nonzero; therefore \(DS=P\). Minimality of \(K\) forces \(D=K\). Consequently
\[
K^4=1,\qquad [K,K]\le Z(K).
\]

It follows that
\[
R:=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]
\]
is central elementary abelian. Put
\[
M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=(K\cap S)/R.
\]
The minimality argument from Turn 79 gives
\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[G/K]}(M),\qquad
M/T_0\simeq V,\qquad
(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0.
\tag{86.2}
\]

### First lift: the entire elementary quotient \(M\)

Finite-Heisenberg deformation duality applies to arbitrary elementary modules, not merely semisimple ones. It gives
\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(M)^\vee\simeq (M^\vee)^{G/K}=0
\]
and hence every generated lower map lifts through \(M\). The number of relation-compatible lifts is
\[
2^{2\dim M}
\]
for both sources. 

A proper lift image retains the fixed tame frame and has strictly smaller marked wild kernel, so its contribution is already equal by induction. Thus the surjective lift counts through \(M\) agree.

### Second lift: the central elementary kernel \(R\)

Let
\[
\mathcal D=(R^\vee)^{G/R}.
\]
Finite-Heisenberg duality identifies \(\mathcal D\) with the full character group of the \(R\)-valued relation-obstruction space.

For a fixed-frame lower-map set \(X_\Gamma\), write
\[
o_\Gamma(\rho)\in\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(R)
\]
for the obstruction and
\[
m_{\Gamma,\lambda}
=
\#\{\rho\in X_\Gamma:\langle\lambda,o_\Gamma(\rho)\rangle=0\}.
\]
Fourier inversion gives
\[
\#\{\rho:o_\Gamma(\rho)=0\}
=
\frac1{|\mathcal D|}
\sum_{\lambda\in\mathcal D}
\bigl(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}-|X_\Gamma|\bigr).
\tag{86.3}
\]

For \(\lambda=0\), equality follows from the already-matched \(G/R\)-count.

For \(\lambda\ne0\), the scalar pushout
\[
K/\ker\lambda
\]
is a nonsplit central extension whose quadratic map vanishes on \(T_0\), has polar form vanishing on \(T_0\times M\), and descends to a nonzero nonsingular determinant form on the simple head \(V\).

Its obstruction has two possibilities.

If the filtration-one edge is nonzero, perfect deformation duality makes it an exact half-split. The radical–Frattini lemma gives
\[
T_0\le \Phi(G/R),
\]
so imposing generation does not disturb the half-half ratio: every lift over the \(T_0\)-quotient is already surjective. 

If the edge is zero, transgression and marking-safe shearing reduce the scalar pushout to the pure determinant problem on \(V\). The candidate Wall–Arf calculation and the local Kummer/deep-unit theorem give identical zero counts: minus type in the unramified case and hyperbolic type in the ramified case.

Therefore
\[
m_{\Gamma_{A_2},\lambda}
=
m_{G_{\mathbb Q_2},\lambda}
\]
for every \(\lambda\in\mathcal D\). Equation (86.3) gives equality of unobstructed lower maps.

Once the \(R\)-obstruction vanishes, finite-Heisenberg duality gives the same \(R\)-lift multiplicity. Every such lift is surjective: if its image \(J\) maps onto \(G/R\), then
\[
(J\cap K)R=K,
\]
and \(R=\Phi(K)\) forces \(J\cap K=K\), hence \(J=G\).

This proves (86.1).

Summing over all tame frames \(\alpha\), taking the decoration trivial, and specializing to
\[
L=O_2(G)
\]
gives
\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{A_2},G)|
=
|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbb Q_2},G)|
\]
for every finite target, conditional only on the stated standard critical-subgroup input and the previously established deformation and local-duality lemmas. This is the verifier’s exact \(2\)-core semantics. 

**ESTABLISHED**: Consolidated Turns 74–85 into one fixed-tame-frame induction theorem. Removed the unsupported \(\Omega _2(C)\)-reduction wording: the proof requires only the direct small-exponent Thompson critical-subgroup theorem. With that input, the scalar terminal case, nonsemisimple elementary lift, central Fourier block, determinant/edge alternatives, and surjectivity are one well-founded induction on the wild kernel.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. A publication-grade submission must give a formal bibliographic citation—or a full proof—of the direct small-exponent critical-subgroup theorem and consolidate the finite-Heisenberg and Wall–Arf/local-Kummer lemmas.

**NEXT**: Turn the theorem above into the standalone proof note, append the unchanged residue submission block, and send that exact block to the official verifier.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
